_provenance.note: This Stage 1 JSON was reconstructed from the currently-published cards so external Stage 2 (Gemini bias) and Stage 3 (Codex fact-check) reviews can verify what readers see. It is not the original analysis — the original pipeline was not run on this issue.

headline: The Clause Nobody Mentioned in the Amendment
context: Parliament passed the Communications Amendment on 20 March 2026 with 148-32 votes. Buried in Section 14A: platforms become liable for user content.

cards.text: Bipartisan support for the Communications Amendment.
cards.sub: Nobody is talking about Section 14A on page 34, which redefines who is responsible for what you post.

cards.h: The term 'reasonable steps' is never defined.
cards.s: MCMC decides what platforms must do, without publishing any standard. Three previous actions overturned by High Court.
cards.d: The term 'reasonable steps' is never defined. MCMC decides what platforms must do, without publishing any standard. Three previous actions overturned by High Court.

cards.h: Indonesia passed the same law. Takedowns rose 43%.
cards.s: Malaysia's bill bypassed the standard 30-day consultation.
cards.d: Indonesia passed the same law. Takedowns rose 43%. Malaysia's bill bypassed the standard 30-day consultation.

cards.h: PAS voted yes. DAP voted yes. Bersatu voted no.
cards.s: This cuts along religious-secular lines, not coalition lines.
cards.d: PAS voted yes. DAP voted yes. Bersatu voted no. This cuts along religious-secular lines, not coalition lines.

cards.text: Like a rental agreement where the landlord can change the locks anytime, and 'reasonable notice' means whatever they decide it means
cards.sub: Platform liability without clear definitions gives regulators blank-cheque enforcement power

cards.text: Good regulation needs clear rules, independent enforcement, and judicial review. This bill provides the third but not the first two.

confidence: Low — derived from published cards, original brief not on disk; submit for retroactive Stage 2/3 verification
